summary: Backend regression for iter181: tested 12 recently-implemented features (branch daily stock count w/ variance and loss distribution, shift-close blocking when count missing, inventory-movements with filters, smart purchase suggestions, approve & convert to invoice, monthly department stocktake, salary receipt print data, biometric-queue/pending, waste-efficiency report, stockout predictions, manufactured-products cost fields). Created /app/backend/tests/test_iter181_recent_features.py (25 tests). 22 passed, 2 skipped (no seed data: empty branch inventory + no payroll rows), 1 failed: legacy manufactured products miss the new before/after-waste cost fields.

minor.issue: Legacy products (created before the cost_before_waste/cost_after_waste feature) are returned without 'production_cost', 'raw_material_cost_after_waste', and 'cost_before_waste' fields. 2 of 3 tenant products are missing these keys, so the frontend product card will show empty 'before/after waste' values for older items. The handler must compute these on-the-fly from the recipe (same formula used in create), or run a one-shot migration.

action_items: GET /api/manufactured-products should back-fill cost_before_waste / raw_material_cost_after_waste / production_cost from the recipe when missing (or migrate legacy docs). Currently only newly-created products carry these fields, so product cards will be incomplete for older manufactured products.

Optional: enforce the 'last 5 days of month' window inside POST /api/department-stock-count/submit (currently the endpoint accepts submissions any day; only the GET /is-due flag drives UI button visibility).
Re-run the variance & salary-receipt tests after seed data is added (currently skipped because tenant has empty branch inventory and zero payroll records).
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is 22,503 lines — must be split into routers (biometric-queue, tenants, etc.).

GET /api/biometric-queue/pending is intentionally unauthenticated (local agent poll) and atomically claims jobs by flipping status to 'processing' — confirmed working. Make sure rate-limiting / token-from-tenant is added before production exposure.
/app/backend/routes/inventory_system.py is 4,135 lines — split waste-efficiency, suggestions and stockout-predictions into their own modules.

branch_stock_count.submit deletes all prior 'branch_loss' movements for the day before reinserting — good idempotency. But it does not also reverse-adjust raw-material warehouse stock for the auto-distributed loss; recipe_breakdown is only stored on the movement, never decremented from raw_materials.quantity. Verify the business expectation.

price-and-create-invoice: raw_material_id back-fill from request-items-by-name works as designed (test passes). Status guard requires 'approved_by_owner' — admin role uses owner-equivalent approval and reached this state in tests.

success_rate.backend: 92% (22/24 executable tests passing; 1 legitimate failure on legacy products, 2 skipped due to missing seed data)

seed_data_creation: Created via API during test run: 1 warehouse_purchase_request (approved & converted to a purchases_new invoice). 1+ branch_stock_counts row for today's date for the admin's primary branch (notes prefixed 'TEST_iter181').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter181_recent_features.py covers all 12 recently-added endpoints. After main agent patches GET /manufactured-products to compute/backfill cost_before_waste & raw_material_cost_after_waste for legacy products, rerun: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter181_recent_features.py::TestManufacturingCost -v. Skipped tests need seed data: (a) at least one branch_inventory row (>0 qty) for the admin branch so the variance test exercises variance/loss-distribution and the recipe-breakdown branch, (b) at least one payroll row so HR receipt print-data is verified. Cash-register close-block test currently passes whether the count was already submitted earlier in the run or not — to deterministically validate the 409+STOCK_COUNT_REQUIRED contract, seed inventory but DO NOT submit a count, then call /api/cash-register/close.
rca of the issue: GET /api/manufactured-products simply returns documents from manufactured_products as stored. The new cost_before_waste / production_cost / raw_material_cost_after_waste keys are only written inside POST /manufactured-products (create). Documents inserted before the feature ship date never receive those keys, so they leak as missing. Fix: in get_manufactured_products(), if a doc lacks these keys, recompute from doc['recipe'] using the same formula (qty * cost_per_unit before waste, qty * cost_per_unit/(1-waste_percentage/100) after waste). Alternatively, add a one-time migration script under /app/backend/seed_data.py or similar to populate the legacy docs.
